paper_title: The Effect of Drying Techniques and Storage Conditions on the Quality of Bee-Collected Pollen

methods.description: This method involves various drying techniques used to preserve freshly collected pollen by reducing moisture content and water activity, which are crucial for extending shelf life and maintaining the quality of bee-collected pollen.

steps.description: Drying the pollen with hot air is recommended at the temperature of 40°C.
steps.searchable_summary: apply hot-air drying at 40°C to pollen samples

steps.description: Freeze-drying is defined as an ideal method for treating the commercially collected pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: apply freeze-drying to commercially collected pollen

steps.description: Microwave drying is an ideal drying technique for the preservation of the bioactive compounds of fresh pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: apply microwave drying to preserve bioactive compounds in pollen

methods.description: This method compares the effectiveness of different drying techniques in preserving the nutritional and bioactive properties of bee-collected pollen.

steps.description: Compare the concentration of total polyphenols in lyophilized and air-dried pollen samples.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze total polyphenols in lyophilized vs air-dried pollen

steps.description: Evaluate the total flavonoid content in lyophilized and air-dried pollen samples.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate flavonoid content in different drying methods

steps.description: Assess antioxidant activity using the ORAC assay in lyophilized and air-dried pollen samples.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ORAC assay for antioxidant activity comparison
